title: Fix Daily Entry draft crash (Minified React error #185 / Maximum update depth exceeded)

requirements.text: Fix the infinite update loop that triggers “Minified React error #185 / Maximum update depth exceeded” on the Daily Entry page in the draft preview, so the Daily Entry page renders normally without showing the generic “Something went wrong” error.
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the draft preview and landing on the Daily Entry page does not throw React error #185.
The app does not display the root-route “Something went wrong” / error fallback when navigating to the Daily Entry page.
The Daily Entry page remains stable (no repeated re-render loop) while idle (no user interaction) for at least 30 seconds.
Restaurant Name select, Balance Date input, and ingredient Balance/Order inputs remain interactive and do not cause crashes when edited.
Save Record button remains clickable (subject to existing validation like required Restaurant Name and Balance Date) and does not trigger an infinite render loop.

requirements.text: Ensure any reactive backend-connection/actor readiness UI state used by the Daily Entry page does not continuously set React state when there is no effective change (e.g., avoid repeatedly writing the same readiness values to state), preventing future maximum update depth regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend readiness/loading/error indicators on the Daily Entry page do not trigger continuous state updates when the underlying actor query state is unchanged.
Toggling backend actor states (loading → ready, ready → error via retry, etc.) updates the UI once per state change without creating a render loop.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only UI component sources).
Do not modify frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts(x), frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, or frontend/src/main.tsx (immutable paths).
Keep the backend as a single Motoko actor (no new backend services).
nonGoals: No new product features or UI redesign beyond what is necessary to stop the crash on the Daily Entry page.
No changes to data model/schema or Motoko backend behavior unless strictly required to address the Daily Entry crash.
